seoTitle: How to Find a Cloudflare Ray ID (2026)
metaDescription: Locate a Cloudflare Ray ID on a blocked page or in browser network headers, then use it in Security Analytics to find which rule caused the block.
intro: A Ray ID is a unique fingerprint attached to a single web request, and it's the fastest way to trace exactly why Cloudflare blocked or challenged a visitor. This guide covers finding a Ray ID and using it to look up the triggering firewall rule.

steps.heading: Check the bottom of a block page
steps.body: If you see a WAF challenge or security error page, scroll to the footer to find the Ray ID string.

steps.heading: Or find it in browser dev tools
steps.body: Open your browser's inspect element panel and look for the 'cf-ray' request or response header on the network tab.

steps.heading: Log into your Cloudflare dashboard
steps.body: Select your target site zone and navigate to the Security directory.

steps.heading: Open Security Analytics
steps.body: Go to the Events analytics sheet within the Security section.

steps.heading: Filter by Ray ID
steps.body: Click Add Filter, set the parameter to 'Ray ID equals', and paste your copied ID to instantly identify which firewall rule caused the block.

faq.question: What exactly is a Cloudflare Ray ID?
faq.answer: It's a unique identifier assigned to a single web request that acts as a diagnostic fingerprint, letting you trace exactly what happened to that specific request.

faq.question: Why would I need to share a Ray ID with a site admin?
faq.answer: If you're a visitor who got blocked, sharing the Ray ID lets the site's administrator quickly look up your exact request in their security logs.

faq.question: Can I find a Ray ID without seeing a block page?
faq.answer: Yes, every request through Cloudflare includes a 'cf-ray' header visible in your browser's developer tools network tab, blocked or not.

faq.question: What information does the Security Analytics filter show once I search a Ray ID?
faq.answer: It shows exactly which firewall rule or challenge triggered the action, giving you the specific reason behind the block.
